title: University of Arkansas: A Great Place to Call Home
body: Graduated from the University of Arkansas in 1986. Oh my, can't believe how much this place has grown! Freshman year, I was stuck in Reid Hall on the 8th floor without AC. One of the first pics shows Reid. Now there's a huge residential complex next to it. This area used to be the tennis courts I played on many days in the humid weather. Walking to my classes was a long haul from Reid Hall. I recall taking history in Peabody Hall, but couldn't find it in these videos. Had to walk from the HYPER building up a step hill to Kimpel Hall for speech class. One things about this campus, if you're not in great shape, you better get into shape fast!  Have so many fond memories of this place. The student enrollment when I attended was 16k. I'm sure it's much bigger now. I haven't been back since, but am hoping to return later this Fall if possible just to see everything. I want to find my name on the senior walk if possible. My husband and I live in Omaha but are really thinking about moving here to enjoy our lives once our kids who just graduated are on their own.  Fayetteville is one of a kind! Just wish my favorite pizza place, King Pizza, still existed!
author: @gemini6828

title: University of Arkansas: A Great Place to Call Home
body:
Had to get a few extra heavey items for a student.
Parking staff closing in 5 min wouldn't let us drop stuff off in the lot by the room and get a cart.
Instead had to bring refrigerator and room stuff to the 5th floor parking garage.
It was so hot in the parking garage and it was packed full.
Everyone helping got sick and almost had heat stroke from the Garland parking garage.
They need a better system.
It could have taken ten minutes intead it took hours and people got heat sick really bad.
Bad first impression.
author: @myfavoriteplanet3247

title: Ultimate Boston Campus Tour #boston #mit #bostoncollege #bu
body: Why should we take you opinion on any of your reviews. What are your qualifications? Who asked you to do this? What’s your major? How long have you been attending the academic institution you’ve been enrolled in? Are you a professional student? So just because you know these institutions of higher learning doesn’t mean you know the bings that are important to me specifically. Thanks, but no thanks!
